data.title: The White House's registration of the "aliens.gov" domain has sparked speculation that Trump is about to reveal UFO secrets.
data.description: The White House has officially registered the domain "aliens.gov," which has appeared in the federal government's official domain name registry. The website currently has no content. Weeks earlier, Trump announced on Truth Social that he planned to declassify government files related to extraterrestrial life and unidentified aerial phenomena, and had instructed the Secretary of Defense and relevant agencies to identify and release all UFO-related documents. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed that the review of these files was underway but did not disclose a specific timeline. Former senior defense intelligence official Christopher Mellon stated that the government possesses a large amount of video evidence that the public has never seen, including videos from the onboard cameras of F-18 fighter jets, some of which were classified as unclassified in 2018 but have not yet been released to the public.

data.title: BTQ Technologies Implements Quantum-Resistant Bitcoin Prototype
data.description: BTQ Technologies has successfully launched a working implementation of BIP 360 on its Bitcoin Quantum testnet. According to NS3.AI, this testnet now includes over 50 miners and has surpassed 100,000 mined blocks. The prototype allows developers to experiment with quantum-resistant Bitcoin transactions. However, for widespread adoption, miners and users would need to transition to a separate blockchain rather than upgrading the existing Bitcoin network.

data.title: India's Power Demand Projected to Reach 459 Gigawatts by 2030
data.description: India's power ministry has projected that the country's maximum power demand will rise to 459 gigawatts by the end of the decade. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the anticipated significant expansion in both generation and storage capacity to meet this demand. The ministry's forecast underscores the need for substantial infrastructure development to support the growing energy requirements. This projection reflects India's ongoing efforts to enhance its energy capabilities in response to increasing consumption patterns. The ministry's outlook suggests a transformative period for the nation's power sector, necessitating strategic investments and advancements in technology to ensure sustainable growth.

data.title: A Los Angeles ride-hailing driver has been charged with using $2 million in COVID-19 relief loans to buy cryptocurrency.
data.description: Bruce Choi, a 34-year-old resident of Koreatown, Los Angeles, was arrested at San Francisco International Airport on Tuesday and charged with wire fraud and money laundering. Federal prosecutors allege that he used a fictitious company, "Premier Republic," to submit fraudulent economic damage disaster loan applications, obtaining approximately $2 million in COVID-19 business relief loans, which he then transferred to accounts on the cryptocurrency exchange Kraken to purchase cryptocurrencies. During the investigation, prosecutors seized nearly 40 BTC and other cryptocurrencies. If convicted, each count of wire fraud carries a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison, and the money laundering charge carries a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison.

data.title: CK Hutchison Focuses on Investment in Stable Legal Environments
data.description:
CK Hutchison Chairman Victor Li stated during an online performance meeting that after selling UK assets, the company will continue to focus on investing in countries that respect contractual agreements and have robust legal systems to protect investments. According to RTHK, Li emphasized the importance of projects with long-term stable cash flows and expressed interest in investing in such opportunities.

The group has fully divested its stake in UK Power Networks, and CK Infrastructure has completed the sale of a 70% stake in UK Rails. Li did not directly address whether a special dividend would be issued, noting that the group's financial position is strong and there is no pressure to sell any assets.

CK Hutchison's Co-Managing Director and Chief Financial Officer Frank Sixt mentioned that the board has not made any decisions regarding transactions in the company's global telecommunications business. He highlighted that the group has received various proposals and will explore and assess all potential opportunities.

Additionally, when asked about the possibility of a dual listing for Watsons, the group stated that no decision has been made regarding such a transaction.

data.title: Crypto.com is laying off approximately 12% of its workforce, with the CEO citing the need for AI transformation.
data.description: Crypto exchange Crypto.com announced it will lay off approximately 12% of its workforce, citing the need to adapt its business to the increasing capabilities of AI. According to the Straits Times, the layoffs affect about 180 employees. Crypto.com CEO Kris Marszalek stated on the X platform that companies that do not immediately undertake this transformation will fail, while those that act immediately and combine the best AI tools with top talent will achieve previously unattainable scale and precision. This layoff is the latest example of cost-cutting in the digital finance industry. Previously, cryptocurrency exchange Gemini Space Station Inc. laid off approximately 25% of its workforce earlier this year and closed its operations in the UK, Europe, and Australia. Jack Dorsey's Block Inc. announced last month that it would lay off nearly half of its workforce, citing its assessment that AI will transform the industry.

data.title: UK Residential Mortgage Options Reach Seven-Month Low
data.description: The availability of residential mortgage products in the UK has decreased to its lowest level in over seven months. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant reduction in mortgage options for potential homebuyers. This decline comes amid ongoing economic challenges and uncertainties affecting the housing market. Analysts suggest that the reduction in mortgage products may impact the ability of individuals to secure financing for home purchases, potentially influencing the overall real estate market dynamics. The situation reflects broader financial trends and concerns within the UK economy, as lenders adjust their offerings in response to market conditions.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Crypto Exchange Reduces Workforce Amid AI Advancements
data.description: A prominent cryptocurrency exchange has announced plans to cut approximately 12% of its staff. Bloomberg posted on X that the decision is driven by the company's need to adjust its operations in response to the growing influence of artificial intelligence technologies. The exchange aims to streamline its business processes and enhance efficiency as AI continues to evolve and impact various sectors. This move reflects a broader trend in the industry, where companies are increasingly integrating AI to optimize their operations and remain competitive.

data.title: Global Agriculture Faces Disruption Amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
The ongoing conflict in Iran is causing significant disruptions in global agriculture, impacting food availability and prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the agricultural sector due to the turmoil. The war has led to uncertainties in supply chains, affecting the production and distribution of essential commodities worldwide.

Experts warn that the instability in the region could lead to increased costs for consumers as the availability of key agricultural products diminishes. The ripple effects of the conflict are being felt across various markets, with potential long-term implications for global food security.

As the situation continues to evolve, stakeholders in the agricultural industry are closely monitoring developments to mitigate the impact on food supply chains. The conflict underscores the interconnectedness of global markets and the vulnerability of agriculture to geopolitical tensions.

data.title: European Banks Downplay Exposure to Private Credit Concerns
data.description: European bank executives have largely dismissed concerns about their exposure to the private credit sector, which is currently facing challenges related to loan quality and a significant withdrawal of investors. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that many bank chiefs described their involvement in private credit as 'nonexistent,' 'marginal,' 'not material,' or 'almost zero.' This response comes amid growing scrutiny of the private credit industry, which has been under pressure due to rising interest rates and economic uncertainties. Despite these challenges, European banks appear confident in their limited exposure, suggesting that the sector's troubles will not significantly impact their operations. The private credit market has been experiencing difficulties as investors reassess their positions, leading to concerns about the stability and future of the industry. However, the reassurances from European banks indicate a belief that their financial health remains robust despite the ongoing issues in private credit.

data.title: Europe Turns to Coal Amid Rising Natural Gas Prices
data.description: Europe is increasingly relying on coal as natural gas prices continue to rise, prompting utilities to seek more affordable alternatives to maintain energy supply. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in energy sources as a response to the economic pressures faced by the region. The surge in natural gas prices has led to a significant increase in coal consumption, as utilities aim to manage costs while ensuring consistent power generation. This trend underscores the challenges faced by European countries in balancing energy needs with environmental commitments. The reliance on coal, a cheaper but less environmentally friendly option, reflects the urgent need for solutions to address the ongoing energy crisis.

data.title: Pan Gongsheng, Governor of the People's Bank of China, met with a delegation led by Paul Chan, Financial Secretary of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government.
data.description: On the afternoon of March 19, 2026, Pan Gongsheng, Governor of the People's Bank of China, met with visiting officials from the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government, including Financial Secretary Paul Chan, Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury Christopher Hui, Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Monetary Authority Eddie Yue, Chairman of the Securities and Futures Commission Wong Tin-yau, and Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission, Leung Fung-yee. The two sides exchanged views on issues of common concern, including the current macroeconomic situation, deepening the interconnection between the mainland and Hong Kong financial markets, strengthening the development of Hong Kong's offshore RMB market, consolidating and enhancing Hong Kong's status as an international financial center, and continuously promoting high-level opening up of the financial sector. Vice Governors of the People's Bank of China Xuan Changneng, Lu Lei, and Zou Lan also attended the meeting. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S.-Israel Divide Widens Over Iran Conflict
data.description:
The United States and Israel are experiencing a growing divergence in their support for the conflict with Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing differences in approach between the two nations. While both countries have historically aligned on many security issues, recent developments have exposed a rift in their strategies and priorities concerning Iran.

The U.S. has been focusing on diplomatic efforts and negotiations to address the situation, whereas Israel has maintained a more aggressive stance, emphasizing military readiness and potential action. This divergence is becoming more pronounced as each country navigates its own domestic and international pressures.

The evolving geopolitical landscape and differing national interests are contributing to this widening gap. As the situation unfolds, the implications for regional stability and international relations remain uncertain.

data.title: Iran launched a retaliatory strike against Israel using a swarm of new-generation suicide drones.
data.description: On March 19, local time, the Iranian military issued a statement saying that in retaliation for the deaths of the crew members of the USS Dena and former intelligence minister Khatib, the Iranian army launched a large-scale drone attack starting in the early hours of that day. The main targets included the Israeli Ministry of State Security and a military camp near the Lebanese and Syrian border. The Iranian statement emphasized that a swarm of new-generation suicide drones was used in this round of attacks. (CCTV News)

data.title: Bob Iger's Return to Disney Falls Short of Shareholder Expectations
data.description: Bob Iger, who returned to Disney to address several challenges, has largely succeeded in his objectives, except in meeting shareholder expectations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while Iger has made significant strides in stabilizing the company, the anticipated financial outcomes have not fully materialized. His leadership has been instrumental in navigating Disney through a complex landscape, yet the financial community remains cautious about the company's future performance. Despite these efforts, the gap between strategic achievements and shareholder satisfaction persists, raising questions about the long-term impact of Iger's tenure.

data.title: White House Urged to Declare Victory in Iran and De-escalate
data.description: The White House is advised to declare victory in Iran and pursue de-escalation strategies. Bloomberg posted on X, suggesting that this approach could be beneficial for the current administration. The recommendation comes amid ongoing tensions and complex geopolitical dynamics involving Iran. The strategy aims to reduce hostilities and promote stability in the region, aligning with broader diplomatic goals. This move could potentially ease international relations and foster a more peaceful environment.

data.title: STOCKS | Bank of America Raises Micron Technology's Target Price to $500
data.description: Bank of America has increased the target price for Micron Technology (MU.O) from $400 to $500. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects the bank's positive outlook on Micron's future performance and market position. The decision comes amid growing optimism in the semiconductor industry, driven by advancements in technology and increased demand for memory products. Micron Technology, a leading provider of memory solutions, is expected to benefit from these industry trends, prompting the upward revision of its target price.

data.title: Lawmakers Debate Social Media Age Restrictions Amid Concerns for Children's Safety
data.description:
Lawmakers are currently deliberating on the implementation of age restrictions for social media platforms, as concerns grow over the potential harm these platforms may pose to children. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing discussions among juries about the impact of social media on young users. The debate centers around the balance between protecting children and preserving their access to digital communication tools.

Recent studies have raised alarms about the negative effects of social media on mental health, prompting calls for stricter regulations. Lawmakers are considering various approaches, including setting minimum age requirements and enhancing parental controls. These measures aim to mitigate risks such as cyberbullying, exposure to inappropriate content, and privacy violations.

The conversation is part of a broader effort to address the challenges posed by digital platforms in today's society. As technology continues to evolve, policymakers are tasked with finding solutions that safeguard young users while allowing them to benefit from the positive aspects of social media. The outcome of these discussions could lead to significant changes in how social media platforms operate and interact with their younger audiences.

data.title: Apple's Stock Decline: Analysts Maintain Optimistic Price Target
data.description: Apple's stock closed at $249.94 on Wednesday, marking a 1.69% decrease as it fell below the $250 mark and broke its 200-day simple moving average. According to NS3.AI, despite this downturn, 24 Wall Street analysts continue to hold an average price target of $304.66 for the tech giant.

data.title: Eli Lilly's Experimental Drug Shows Promising Weight Loss Results in Diabetic Patients
data.description: Eli Lilly's experimental medication has demonstrated significant weight loss in diabetic patients, surpassing existing treatments. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the drug's potential impact on the market. The results from clinical trials indicate that this new treatment could offer a more effective solution for weight management in individuals with diabetes. The promising outcomes have generated considerable interest in the pharmaceutical industry, as Eli Lilly continues to advance its research and development efforts. The company aims to address the growing demand for innovative therapies that can improve the quality of life for diabetic patients.
